title: fraxinus americana subsp. pennsylvanica
intro: Fraxinus americana subsp. pennsylvanica, also known as the American green ash, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its smooth, gray bark and compound leaves with 7-13 leaflets. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to exhibit potential in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of American green ash include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Fraxinus americana subsp. pennsylvanica include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
